description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden shingled roof supported by four sturdy wooden posts. The well features a wooden crank mechanism with a rope and a metal bucket attached, used for drawing water. Ivy or climbing plants are seen growing up one side of the well, adding a touch of greenery and age. The stone base is octagonal and set on a paved stone platform, which is slightly weathered and mossy. The roof is pitched and covered with overlapping wooden shingles, providing shelter for the well opening. A wooden bucket rests on the stone edge, and the overall construction suggests durability and rustic charm.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden shingled roof, crank mechanism, and bucket, surrounded by climbing ivy and set on a mossy stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's stone base, wooden roof, crank mechanism, and a bucket hanging from the rope, with ivy visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden roof structure, climbing ivy, and partial visibility of the crank and bucket behind the post.
description_view_2: Rear view with the crank mechanism and bucket more obscured, ivy climbing up the left side, and the stone base and roof clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden roof support, stone base, and a bucket resting on the well's edge, with less visible ivy.
attribute_well_cover_design: No cover present; the well opening is exposed beneath the wooden roof.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A visible wooden crank mechanism with a rope and metal bucket is present, mounted at the center under the roof.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall approximately waist-high and covered by a pitched wooden roof supported by four posts.
attribute_decorative_elements: Climbing ivy is present on one side, but there are no other decorative elements.
